rationale.R1.2: The artifact exhibits a prominent logical gap: indistinguishability on visual velocity-magnitude fields is treated as sufficient evidence for full physical faithfulness and solver substitutability; this inferential leap is a central consistency failure that any reading of 'What do you think?' plausibly targets.
rationale.R1.3: The discussion's recommendations (drop-in solver replacement, adoption as default surrogate) substantially outrun the evidence, which covers only a single benchmark, one flow variable, and a perceptual discrimination task; claim-to-evidence alignment is materially deficient.
rationale.R1.7: The artifact misattributes Krishnapriyan et al. (2021) — that work documented failure modes and pathological convergence in PINNs, not robustness guarantees; invoking it as a convergence warrant is a significant scholarship error that warrants review regardless of the user's precise intent.
